intro: Cestichis platybulba, also known as the "Australian licorice vine," is a type of plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Australia and Papua New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which may aid in relieving symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Cestichis platybulba include glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cestichis platybulba is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, with the most common preparations being a decoction of the roots and leaves for gastrointestinal issues and a topical application of the plant extracts for skin conditions.
